context: Checks whether Reflect routes durable learnings across all relevant persistence surfaces instead of defaulting to memory.

checklist.name: Multiple surfaces considered
checklist.description: The report explicitly considers instruction files, memory, semantic memory, OpenSpec artifacts, skills, hooks/git hooks, docs/runbooks, and CI or local verification gates.

checklist.name: Target rationale included
checklist.description: Each recommended update explains why that target is better than nearby alternatives.

checklist.name: Automation promoted
checklist.description: Repeated missed verification is routed to enforceable hooks or lifecycle checks when feasible, not only to memory.

checklist.name: Reviewer rule routed
checklist.description: The independent-reviewer learning is routed to the verify workflow or skill instructions, with memory only as secondary context if used.

checklist.name: Generated artifact side effect routed
checklist.description: The dirty generated-assets finding is treated as tool/project behavior and routed to a runbook, skill, hook guard, or memory with a clear confidence caveat.

checklist.name: No low-value persistence
checklist.description: One-off timestamps, PR numbers, and transient branch state are skipped or marked as non-durable.

checklist.name: Evidence required
checklist.description: The report cites user corrections, hook messages, command failures, or changed files as evidence for each candidate.

checklist.name: Actionable output
checklist.description: The report separates apply-now, propose-for-review, and do-not-persist items.
